name: Finnhub
what_it_does: Financial data API providing real-time stock quotes, fundamental data, earnings, news, and alternative data (insider trading, ESG scores) via REST and WebSocket, with a generous free tier.
use_cases: Real-time stock price monitoring via WebSocket for event-driven financial agents
Earnings calendar and earnings surprise data for trading strategy automation
Company news and sentiment analysis for fundamental research agents
Insider trading and institutional ownership data for alternative data signals
Crypto exchange rates and OHLCV data for multi-asset portfolio agents
not_for: Options chains and derivatives data (limited coverage)
Ultra-low latency HFT trading (not a direct market feed)
Global macroeconomic datasets at depth (use Quandl/NASDAQ Data Link)
best_when: You want a generous free tier with WebSocket support for real-time data, plus decent fundamental and alternative data coverage — good for prototyping and medium-scale production use.
avoid_when: You need institutional-grade data quality guarantees, options data, or fixed income coverage.

auth.notes: Single API key, can be passed as header (X-Finnhub-Token) or query parameter. Free tier key immediately available. WebSocket auth also via query parameter on connection URL.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 60 requests/minute, US stocks only, basic fundamentals
pricing.paid_tiers: Starter $0/month (same as free); All-In-One from $19.99/month for global data and higher limits
pricing.notes: Free tier is genuinely competitive — 60 req/min is usable for many agent workflows. Premium plans add global exchanges, more fundamentals, and higher WebSocket limits.

performance.rate_limits: Free: 60 requests/minute; paid plans up to 300+ requests/minute

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns proper HTTP error codes (429 for rate limits, 403 for auth errors). Error response JSON includes descriptive messages. Better error handling than Alpha Vantage.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All REST operations are read-only GET requests — fully idempotent. WebSocket streams are stateless per connection.
known_agent_gotchas: WebSocket requires handling reconnection logic — connection drops are common and not auto-retried by SDK
Free tier data is US stocks only — global coverage requires paid subscription
Real-time data via WebSocket is trades, not quotes — must compute best bid/ask from trade stream
Some alternative data (ESG, lobbying) has significant data quality variation
Symbol format varies by endpoint — US stocks use ticker only, crypto uses BINANCE:BTCUSDT format
Rate limit tracking requires monitoring your own request count — no remaining-calls header on free tier

security_score_components.security_notes: Single API key. Financial data vendor agreement compliance required. Free tier: 60 API calls/minute. Market data redistribution restrictions — read vendor terms carefully for agent use cases.
